name: Data Retention Declaration Gate
description: Any system that stores user data must declare a data retention period or deletion policy. The absence of a retention declaration is a governance gap. This gate is distinct from HG-SPEC-05 — it fires when no retention is declared at all. HG-SPEC-05 fires when contradictory retention requirements exist.

trigger.condition: requirements describe storing, persisting, retaining, or archiving user data
trigger.rationale: Systems that store user data without a declared retention period create regulatory and data minimisation risks.

pass_conditions: retention_policy field is present and non-empty
retention_policy names a specific period, trigger, or policy (not vague)
retention_policy does not match any vague_language_pattern
fail_conditions: User data storage requirement present AND retention_policy is null or missing
User data storage requirement present AND retention_policy is vague

context_schema.retention_policy: string|null — e.g. '90 days from last activity', 'deleted on account closure', '7 years per regulatory requirement'

resolution_required: retention_policy declared with a specific period, event trigger, or regulatory reference
Policy must be unambiguous enough to implement (e.g. '30 days post account closure', '7 years per FCA rules')

fail.reason: User data stored, no retention policy declared

fail.retention_policy: data will be retained for as long as necessary

pass.retention_policy: 90 days from last account activity, then permanently deleted

pass.reason: Gate not triggered — no user data stored
